key_insight: An LLM can act as an offline research agent to iteratively improve spacecraft control policies, while an embedded credibility layer audits each claim against measured seed noise before it is credited.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical, auditable workflow for developing deployable autonomy policies for spacecraft guidance, navigation, and control without letting the LLM operate the vehicle.
method: LLM reads a plain-language problem description and run history
LLM proposes one script edit per iteration, then the system trains and logs the result
Each result must pass seed-noise calibration, reseeded verification of the best run, and leave-one-out pruning of agent edits
evidence_signal: Across a Clohessy-Wiltshire rendezvous task and a safety-constrained docking task, the audited policy exceeded measured seed noise by many standard deviations; on docking, the learned policy remained feasible on every seed while undirected search failed to find any feasible policy.
watch_item: The approach depends on accurate seed-noise estimation and robust audit bookkeeping; if either is mis-specified, the credibility guarantees could be overstated.
paper_title: Agentic AutoResearch forSpace Autonomy: An Auditable, LLM-Driven Research Agent for Aerospace Control Problems
